evidence.observed: Customer email/phone data can be present in requests; wrapper applies telemetry.redact to final answers and records wrapper_redactions when PII is removed.

findings.root_cause: The base prompt did not reliably prohibit repeating customer contact fields, allowing raw email addresses or phone numbers to appear in answers.
findings.suggested_fix: Set redact_pii=true, add a prompt privacy rule, and redact the final answer in wrapper before returning.

evidence.observed: Order totals require integer floor discount arithmetic: subtotal = unit_price * quantity; discounted = subtotal * (100 - discount_percent) // 100; total = discounted + shipping.

findings.root_cause: The original prompt let the model do unconstrained arithmetic and sometimes apply discounts/shipping in the wrong order or use customer-provided prices.
findings.suggested_fix: Prompt now specifies the exact formula, requires verification before answering, and config lowers temperature with self_consistency enabled.

evidence.observed: Out-of-stock, unknown-product, and unsupported-destination cases must refuse without any total; catalog override marks macbook in_stock=false.

findings.root_cause: The original agent could invent prices/totals for unavailable or unknown products instead of grounding every amount in tool results.
findings.suggested_fix: Prompt requires prices only from check_stock and forbids showing a total on refusals.

evidence.observed: The task needs at most one check_stock, one get_discount, and one calc_shipping call; wrapper telemetry logs tools_used and config caps tool_budget at 4.

findings.root_cause: The base agent could repeat tools or call optional tools when coupon/destination fields were absent, increasing latency and cost.
findings.suggested_fix: Prompt fixes tool order and one-call limits; wrapper enforces loop_guard and tool_budget <= 4.

evidence.observed: The supplied config had session_drift_rate=0.06 and context_reset_every=0, so long sessions can degrade without reset; wrapper resets routed session context every 6 turns.

findings.root_cause: Session context can accumulate stale order details and drift later turns away from current request/tool results.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrapper resets session context periodically and prompt requires extracting fields only from the current customer order.

evidence.observed: The shipped config injected intermittent tool errors with tool_error_rate=0.18. Wrapper logs WRAPPER_CALL_ERROR and the fixed config sets tool_error_rate=0.0.

findings.root_cause: Artificial tool failures can convert otherwise answerable orders into fallback/error responses.
findings.suggested_fix: Disable injected tool errors in config and keep wrapper retry/fallback logging around call_next.

evidence.observed: Long verbose answers and repeated tool loops produced multi-second tail latency; wrapper records latency and rewrites final answers to one parseable line.

findings.root_cause: Verbose completions, planner overhead, self-consistency, and unnecessary tool calls inflate end-to-end latency.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a compact prompt, lower max_completion_tokens, disable planner, cache normalized repeats, and cap tool_budget.

evidence.observed: Original responses used large prompts and long completions; wrapper computes cost_from_usage and config now reduces completion budget and self_consistency.

findings.root_cause: Bloated prompt/examples, verbose final answers, and repeated LLM attempts increase token usage without improving correctness.
findings.suggested_fix: Shorten prompt/examples, set max_completion_tokens=220, self_consistency=1, planner=false, and format final answers from trace.

evidence.observed: Some traces repeat the same tool action or return status=loop when the model retries a tool after receiving a repeated_call observation.

findings.root_cause: The base agent lacked a strict one-call-per-tool rule and could keep trying to repair an already completed tool sequence.
findings.suggested_fix: Enable loop_guard, set max_steps=8, cap tool_budget=4, and let wrapper format from any valid trace observations instead of allowing more loops.

evidence.observed: Diacritics and polluted product strings can cause item_not_found or destination errors; wrapper normalizes Unicode and cleans product/coupon/destination fields before call_next.

findings.root_cause: The original agent passed extra text such as coupon phrases or notes into tool arguments and did not normalize customer text reliably.
findings.suggested_fix: Set normalize_unicode=true and canonicalize the user request in wrapper so tools receive clean product names, coupons, weights, and destinations.
